description: Minimalist epub reader with sentence-level TTS, focus mode, and progression tracking

techStack.frontend: React 19 + Vite + TypeScript + Radix UI + SCSS

userStories.title: Scaffold monorepo with pnpm workspaces
userStories.description: Set up the project skeleton: root package.json with pnpm workspaces, client (React+Vite) and server (Fastify) packages, shared types package, TypeScript configs.
acceptanceCriteria: Root package.json with pnpm workspaces pointing to client/, server/, shared/
client/ has React 19 + Vite + TypeScript scaffolded (vite create react-ts or manual). Radix UI and sass installed as dependencies

server/ has Fastify + TypeScript with tsx for dev. Dependencies: fastify, @fastify/multipart, @fastify/static, @fastify/cors, better-sqlite3, uuid, epub2 (for epub parsing), sbd (sentence boundary detection). Dev deps: tsx, typescript, @types/better-sqlite3, @types/uuid, @types/sbd
shared/ has a types.ts exporting shared interfaces (Book, Chapter, Sentence, Progress)
Root tsconfig.json with project references to all three packages
Each package has its own tsconfig.json with proper extends and paths
pnpm install succeeds without errors
npx tsc --noEmit passes in root (or each package individually)
client dev server starts with pnpm --filter client dev
server dev server starts with pnpm --filter server dev (can just log 'server started' for now)
userStories.notes: Use epub2 package (not epubjs) for server-side epub parsing. The shared package should export types only, no runtime code.

userStories.title: Database schema and initialization
userStories.description: Create SQLite database with tables for books, chapters, sentences, and progress. Initialize on server startup.
acceptanceCriteria: server/src/db.ts exports a function that initializes SQLite DB at data/tts-reader.db
Creates data/ directory if it doesn't exist

Creates tables with IF NOT EXISTS: books (id TEXT PK, title TEXT, author TEXT, cover_path TEXT, file_path TEXT, created_at INTEGER), chapters (id TEXT PK, book_id TEXT FK, idx INTEGER, title TEXT), sentences (id TEXT PK, chapter_id TEXT FK, idx INTEGER, text TEXT), progress (book_id TEXT PK FK, sentence_id TEXT FK, updated_at INTEGER)
Indexes on: chapters(book_id), sentences(chapter_id), progress(book_id)
DB is initialized when server starts (call init in server entry point)
Server starts successfully and creates the DB file
TypeScript compiles without errors
userStories.notes: Use INTEGER for timestamps (unix epoch). Use TEXT for UUIDs. The data/ directory should be gitignored.

userStories.title: Epub upload and parsing endpoint
userStories.description: POST /api/books endpoint that accepts epub file upload, parses it into chapters and sentences, stores everything in DB and filesystem.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/books accepts multipart file upload (epub file)
Saves raw epub to data/books/{id}.epub
Parses epub using epub2: extracts title, author, cover image
Cover image saved to data/covers/{id}.jpg (or png) if present
Extracts chapters in order with titles from epub TOC/spine
Each chapter's HTML content is stripped to plain text
Plain text is split into sentences using sbd library
All data inserted into DB: book record, chapter records (with idx for ordering), sentence records (with idx for ordering)
Returns created book object as JSON: { id, title, author, coverPath, totalChapters, totalSentences }
Returns 400 if file is not an epub or is missing
TypeScript compiles without errors
Test with a real epub file: curl -F 'file=@test.epub' http://localhost:3000/api/books returns valid JSON
userStories.notes: epub2 provides getChapter() for HTML content. Use a simple HTML-to-text approach (regex strip tags or a lightweight lib). sbd.sentences(text) returns array of sentences. Filter out empty sentences. Register @fastify/multipart plugin.

userStories.title: Book listing and detail API endpoints
userStories.description: GET /api/books (list all) and GET /api/books/:id (single book with chapters) and DELETE /api/books/:id.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/books returns array of books with fields: id, title, author, coverPath, createdAt, totalChapters, totalSentences, progress (current sentence index / total sentences, or null if no progress)
GET /api/books/:id returns book with nested chapters array: { id, title, author, coverPath, chapters: [{ id, idx, title, sentenceCount }] }
GET /api/books/:id returns 404 if book not found
DELETE /api/books/:id removes book, all chapters, all sentences, progress from DB. Removes epub file and cover from disk
DELETE /api/books/:id returns 204 on success, 404 if not found
TypeScript compiles without errors
userStories.notes: For the book list, compute progress as a percentage using a JOIN or subquery. Cover path should be relative (e.g., /api/covers/{id}.jpg).

userStories.title: Chapter sentences and progress API endpoints
userStories.description: GET /api/chapters/:id (all sentences for a chapter), GET/PUT /api/books/:id/progress.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/chapters/:id returns { id, bookId, idx, title, sentences: [{ id, idx, text }] } ordered by idx
GET /api/chapters/:id returns 404 if chapter not found
GET /api/books/:id/progress returns { bookId, sentenceId, chapterId, chapterIdx, sentenceIdx, updatedAt } or 404 if no progress saved
PUT /api/books/:id/progress accepts JSON body { sentenceId } and upserts progress record
PUT /api/books/:id/progress returns the updated progress object (same shape as GET)
Returns 400 if sentenceId is missing or invalid (doesn't exist in DB)
TypeScript compiles without errors
userStories.notes: Progress GET should resolve the chapter info from the sentence's chapter_id so client knows which chapter to load.

userStories.title: Static file serving for covers and SPA
userStories.description: Serve cover images and the built client SPA from Fastify.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/covers/:filename serves files from data/covers/ directory
Serves with correct Content-Type (image/jpeg, image/png)
Returns 404 for missing covers
In production mode, serves client/dist/ as static files for all non-/api routes (SPA fallback)
In development mode, does not serve static files (Vite dev server handles it)
@fastify/static registered for cover serving
@fastify/cors registered for development (allow localhost:5173)
TypeScript compiles without errors
userStories.notes: Use @fastify/static with wildcard option for SPA fallback. CORS only needed in dev when client runs on different port.

userStories.title: Client: routing and layout shell
userStories.description: Set up React Router with two routes: Library (/) and Reader (/read/:bookId). Create the app shell layout.
acceptanceCriteria: react-router-dom installed and configured with BrowserRouter
Route / renders LibraryPage component
Route /read/:bookId renders ReaderPage component
Both pages render placeholder content for now
Global SCSS with CSS reset, base font (system font stack), dark-friendly color variables
App.tsx wraps routes in layout
API client service created at client/src/services/api.ts with typed fetch wrapper functions for all endpoints (listBooks, getBook, getChapter, uploadBook, deleteBook, getProgress, updateProgress)
API base URL configurable (defaults to '' in production, 'http://localhost:3000' in dev via env var)
TypeScript compiles without errors
Client dev server renders without errors
userStories.notes: Keep it simple — no state management library. React state + context is sufficient. Use shared types from the shared package for API response types.

userStories.title: Client: Library page with book grid
userStories.description: Library page showing grid of book cards with cover, title, author, progress bar. Fetches from API on mount.
acceptanceCriteria: Fetches book list from GET /api/books on mount
Shows loading state while fetching
Renders grid of book cards (CSS grid, responsive: 2-4 columns depending on viewport)
Each card shows: cover image (or colored placeholder if no cover), title, author, progress bar with percentage
Colored placeholder generated from title string (deterministic color from hash)
Clicking a card navigates to /read/:bookId
Empty state shown when no books: 'No books yet. Upload an epub to get started.'
Styled with SCSS modules, uses Radix UI components where appropriate
TypeScript compiles without errors
userStories.notes: Progress bar is a simple div with width%. Use CSS grid with auto-fill/minmax for responsive grid. No need for infinite scroll — personal library will be small.

userStories.title: Client: Book upload functionality
userStories.description: Add button to upload epub file from library page. Triggers file picker, uploads to API, refreshes library.
acceptanceCriteria: [+ Add] button visible in library page header
Clicking opens native file picker filtered to .epub files
On file select, uploads to POST /api/books with multipart form data
Shows upload progress or loading spinner during upload
On success, refreshes book list (new book appears in grid)
On error, shows error message (e.g., toast or inline error)
Uses Radix UI Button component
TypeScript compiles without errors
userStories.notes: Keep it simple — hidden file input triggered by button click. No drag-drop needed for v1.

userStories.title: Client: Book delete functionality
userStories.description: Add ability to delete a book from the library with confirmation.
acceptanceCriteria: Right-click on book card shows context menu with 'Delete' option (use Radix UI ContextMenu)
Clicking Delete shows confirmation dialog (Radix UI AlertDialog): 'Delete {title}? This cannot be undone.'
Confirming calls DELETE /api/books/:id and removes card from grid
Cancel dismisses dialog without action
TypeScript compiles without errors
userStories.notes: Radix UI ContextMenu and AlertDialog are the right primitives here.

userStories.title: Client: Reader page - sentence display and navigation
userStories.description: Reader page that loads a book's chapter and displays sentences in focused view. Current sentence large and centered, prev/next dimmed.
acceptanceCriteria: On mount: fetch book info (GET /api/books/:id) and progress (GET /api/books/:id/progress)
If progress exists, load that chapter and set sentence index. If not, load first chapter, first sentence
Fetch current chapter sentences (GET /api/chapters/:chapterId)
Display current sentence: large, full opacity, centered vertically in viewport
Display previous sentence above: smaller font, opacity 0.3
Display next sentence below: smaller font, opacity 0.3
Minimal header: back button (navigates to /), book title, chapter indicator (e.g., 'Ch 3/12')
Bottom controls bar with: previous button, play/pause button, next button
Previous/next buttons change current sentence index
When reaching end of chapter, auto-load next chapter's sentences and continue
When reaching start of chapter on prev, load previous chapter's last sentence
Sentence transitions animate with subtle vertical slide/fade (CSS transition)
TypeScript compiles without errors
Navigation between sentences works correctly
userStories.notes: Use React state for currentChapterIdx and currentSentenceIdx. Preload adjacent chapters for smooth transitions. Use Radix UI IconButton for controls.

userStories.title: Client: TTS integration with Web Speech API
userStories.description: useTTS hook that speaks the current sentence and auto-advances. Play/pause controls.
acceptanceCriteria: useTTS custom hook in client/src/hooks/useTTS.ts
Hook exposes: play(), pause(), isPlaying, speed, setSpeed
play() creates SpeechSynthesisUtterance for current sentence text and calls speechSynthesis.speak()
On utterance 'end' event, advances to next sentence and speaks it (auto-advance loop)
pause() calls speechSynthesis.cancel() and stops auto-advance
Speed control: 0.5x to 2.0x in 0.25 increments, applied via utterance.rate
Play/pause button in reader controls toggles TTS
When user manually navigates (prev/next) while playing, cancel current utterance and speak new sentence
TTS stops when reaching end of last chapter
TypeScript compiles without errors
userStories.notes: Web Speech API quirk: speechSynthesis.cancel() must be called before speaking a new utterance, otherwise they queue. Use speechSynthesis.speaking to check state.

userStories.title: Client: Progress saving
userStories.description: Auto-save reading position to server as user reads.
acceptanceCriteria: On sentence change, debounce a PUT /api/books/:id/progress call (5 second debounce)
Also save immediately on pause and on page unload (beforeunload event)
On returning to a book, resume from saved position (already partly implemented in US-011)
Progress bar on library page reflects actual position
TypeScript compiles without errors
userStories.notes: Use a simple debounce utility (write one, don't install lodash). beforeunload handler should use navigator.sendBeacon for reliability.

userStories.title: Client: Keyboard shortcuts
userStories.description: Global keyboard shortcuts for playback control in reader view.
acceptanceCriteria: Space: toggle play/pause (prevent default scroll)
ArrowLeft: previous sentence
ArrowRight: next sentence
[ (left bracket): decrease speed by 0.25 (min 0.5)
] (right bracket): increase speed by 0.25 (max 2.0)
Shortcuts only active on reader page, not library
Speed change shows brief visual feedback (current speed displayed near controls)
TypeScript compiles without errors
userStories.notes: useEffect with keydown listener. Clean up on unmount. Check that focus isn't in an input before handling.

userStories.title: Client: Focus mode toggle
userStories.description: Toggle to hide surrounding sentences, showing only the current one.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle button/switch in reader controls or header
When focus mode ON: only current sentence visible, prev/next hidden
When focus mode OFF: prev/next sentences visible and dimmed (default)
Toggle state persisted in localStorage
Smooth transition when toggling (fade in/out surrounding sentences)
Uses Radix UI Switch or Toggle component
TypeScript compiles without errors
userStories.notes: Simple boolean state with CSS transition on opacity.

userStories.title: Client: Chapter navigation
userStories.description: Chapter list/TOC panel to jump between chapters.
acceptanceCriteria: Chapter indicator in header is clickable, opens chapter list panel
Panel shows all chapters with titles and index numbers
Current chapter highlighted
Clicking a chapter loads its sentences and jumps to first sentence
Panel closes after selection
Uses Radix UI Dialog or Popover for the panel
If TTS is playing, stop it when jumping chapters
TypeScript compiles without errors
userStories.notes: A simple Radix Dialog with a scrollable list is enough. No need for a sidebar.

userStories.title: Docker and docker-compose setup
userStories.description: Multi-stage Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml for self-hosting.
acceptanceCriteria: Dockerfile with multi-stage build: stage 1 installs deps + builds client (pnpm build in client/), stage 2 copies built client + server code into slim Node.js image
Production server serves client/dist as static files
docker-compose.yml with single service, port 3000 exposed, data/ volume mounted
.dockerignore excludes node_modules, data/, .git, etc.
docker compose build succeeds
docker compose up starts the app and it's accessible at localhost:3000
Uploads persist across container restarts (data volume)
TypeScript compiles without errors
userStories.notes: Use node:22-slim as base. pnpm via corepack. Build client with pnpm --filter client build. Server runs with node (compiled JS) or tsx in production.

userStories.title: Polish: error handling and edge cases
userStories.description: Handle common error cases gracefully across the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Upload of non-epub file shows clear error message
Network errors on API calls show user-friendly error (not raw error)
Empty chapters (no sentences) are skipped during reading
Very long sentences don't break layout (text wraps, max-width applied)
Speed selector shows current value (e.g., '1.0x') in the controls
Back button from reader goes to library
Page title updates to book title when reading
TypeScript compiles without errors
userStories.notes: Light polish pass. Don't over-engineer — just handle the obvious failure modes.

userStories.title: Client: Voice selection for TTS
userStories.description: Let users pick from available browser TTS voices via a dropdown in the reader controls.
acceptanceCriteria: useTTS hook exposes voices list, selectedVoice, and setVoice
Voices populated via speechSynthesis.getVoices() with voiceschanged event listener
Selected voice applied to SpeechSynthesisUtterance before speaking
Voice selection persisted in localStorage (by voice name + lang)
Voice picker dropdown in reader controls bar (Radix Select or native select)
Voices grouped or labeled by language for clarity
If saved voice no longer available, falls back to default
Changing voice while playing restarts current utterance with new voice
TypeScript compiles without errors
userStories.notes: speechSynthesis.getVoices() loads async in some browsers — must listen for voiceschanged event. Persist by voice URI or name+lang combo since voice objects aren't serializable.
